title: cyrtandra kealiae subsp. urceolata
intro: Cyrtandra kealiae subsp. urceolata, commonly known as the Waianae gnatcatcher plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is a small shrub native to the island of Oahu in Hawaii, known for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are believed to aid in reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.
